            Autosys Training Portal
            • Command Line & Web UI
          

          
Learn how to define jobs, build calendars, design box workflows, use REST, and troubleshoot like a production engineer.

        

        

          🧠 Concept → JIL → Web UI → REST
          🏁 Goal: Interview‑ready & production‑ready
        

      


      

        
        

          

            

              

                🌌
                Overview & Core Architecture
              

              

                Autosys is an enterprise job scheduler and workload automation engine. It coordinates jobs across distributed systems, enforces dependencies, and provides centralized visibility into batch processing.
              

            

            

              🏛️ Event Server • Event Processor • Agent
              🧠 JIL • WCC • REST
            

          


          

            

              

                

                  🏗️
                  Core Components
                

                Architecture
              

              

                
Event Server (Database) – stores job definitions, job status, machine information, calendars, and events.

                
Event Processor (EP) – the scheduling brain that reads events, evaluates conditions, updates status, and triggers jobs.

                
Agent (Remote Agent) – runs on target machines, executes commands, monitors jobs, and reports results back to the Event Processor.

                
💡 Think of Autosys as “database + brain + hands” — Event Server, Event Processor, and Agents working together.

                

                  Mental Model
                  # Autosys = Central brain + distributed workers

                  - Event Server  → job catalog & history

                  - Event Processor → scheduler & decision engine

                  - Agent          → executes scripts on machines
                

              

            


            

              

                

                  🧭
                  How This Portal Trains You
                

                Learning Flow
              

              

                
This portal is structured like a hands‑on training lab. Each topic is covered in two parallel paths:

                
    
                  
  • Command Line (JIL & CLI) – how to define and control jobs via scripts.
    • 
                  
  • Web UI (WCC) – how to perform the same tasks visually.
    • 
                

                
You’ll see how to:

                
    
                  
  • Define command, file watcher, and box jobs.
    • 
                  
  • Build calendars and date conditions.
    • 
                  
  • Design box workflows and dependencies.
    • 
                  
  • Use REST for CI/CD and automation.
    • 
                  
  • Interpret statuses and troubleshoot real‑world issues.
    • 
                

                
🎮 Treat each section as a level: understand the concept, study the JIL, mirror it in WCC, then test yourself in the quiz.

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              

                🧩
                Job Types & Job Definitions (JIL & Web UI)
              

              

                A job definition describes what to run, where to run it, when to run it, and how it behaves. Autosys stores all job definitions in its Event Server database.
              

            

            

              ⌨️ JIL – script‑based
              🖱️ WCC – point & click
            

          


          

            

              

                

                  ⌨️
                  Command Path – Command Job (JIL)
                

                insert_job
              

              

                
A command job runs a script or executable on a specified machine. This is the most common job type.

                
Example JIL for a daily ETL command job:

                

                  JIL – Command Job
                  insert_job: DAILY_ETL_JOB

                  job_type: c

                  command: /opt/scripts/run_etl.sh

                  machine: server01

                  owner: etluser@domain

                  date_conditions: 1

                  start_times: "06:00"

                  run_calendar: workdays

                  description: "Daily ETL load job"
                

                
Key attributes:

                
    
                  
  • job_type: c – command job.
    • 
                  
  • command – script or program to execute.
    • 
                  
  • machine – Autosys agent host.
    • 
                  
  • owner – OS user that runs the job.
    • 
                  
  • date_conditions, start_times, run_calendar – schedule.
    • 
                

                
🧪 Use jil -V syntax < my_job.jil to validate JIL syntax without saving it to the database.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🖱️
                  Web UI Path – Create Command Job in WCC
                

                Job Definition Screen
              

              

                
To create the same command job in the Web UI (WCC):

                
    
                  
  • Open WCC and go to Job Activity or Quick View.
    • 
                  
  • Click New → Job.
    • 
                  
  • Select Job Type: Command.
    • 
                  
  • In the General tab:
                    
      
                      
    • Job Name: DAILY_ETL_JOB
      • 
                      
    • Machine: server01
      • 
                      
    • Command: /opt/scripts/run_etl.sh
      • 
                      
    • Owner: etluser
      • 
                    
    
                  
    • 
                  
  • Go to the Schedule tab:
                    
      
                      
    • Enable Date Conditions.
      • 
                      
    • Add Start Time: 06:00.
      • 
                      
    • Select Run Calendar: workdays.
      • 
                    
    
                  
    • 
                  
  • Click Save → Commit.
    • 
                

                
🎨 Every field in WCC maps directly to a JIL attribute. Use the UI to learn the structure, then automate with JIL.

              

            

          


          

            

              

                

                  🕵️‍♂️
                  File Watcher & Box Jobs – JIL
                

                Watchers & Containers
              

              

                
A file watcher job waits for a file to appear or stabilize before triggering downstream processing.

                
File Watcher Job:

                

                  JIL – File Watcher
                  insert_job: FILE_WATCH_INBOUND

                  job_type: f

                  machine: server01

                  watch_file: /data/inbound/orders.csv

                  watch_interval: 60

                  description: "Wait for inbound orders file"
                

                
A box job is a container that groups related jobs and controls their schedule and lifecycle.

                
Box Job:

                

                  JIL – Box Job
                  insert_job: DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX

                  job_type: b

                  date_conditions: 1

                  run_calendar: workdays

                  start_times: "06:00"

                  description: "Daily processing workflow box"
                

                
📦 Box jobs do not run commands themselves; they control when and how child jobs run.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🧭
                  Web UI – File Watcher & Box Jobs
                

                Visual Flows
              

              

                
File Watcher in WCC:

                
    
                  
  • New → Job → Job Type: File Watcher.
    • 
                  
  • General tab:
                    
      
                      
    • Job Name: FILE_WATCH_INBOUND
      • 
                      
    • Machine: server01
      • 
                    
    
                  
    • 
                  
  • File Watcher tab:
                    
      
                      
    • Watch File: /data/inbound/orders.csv
      • 
                      
    • Interval: 60 seconds.
      • 
                    
    
                  
    • 
                  
  • Schedule tab: configure Date Conditions and Start Time if needed.
    • 
                  
  • Save → Commit.
    • 
                


                
Box Job in WCC:

                
    
                  
  • New → Job → Job Type: Box.
    • 
                  
  • General tab: Job Name DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX.
    • 
                  
  • Schedule tab: Date Conditions ON, Run Calendar workdays, Start Time 06:00.
    • 
                  
  • Save → Commit.
    • 
                  
  • For each child job, set Box Name to DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX.
    • 
                

                
🕸️ Use the WCC job flow view to visualize how file watchers, command jobs, and box jobs connect.

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              

                📅
                Calendars & Date/Time Conditions
              

              

                Autosys does not use cron syntax. Instead, it uses start_times, run_calendar, exclude_calendar, and date_conditions to control when jobs run.
              

            

            

              ⏰ start_times
              📆 run_calendar / exclude_calendar
            

          


          

            

              

                

                  ⌨️
                  Command Path – JIL Date Conditions
                

                Calendars & Jobs
              

              

                
There are three main pieces to Autosys date logic:

                
    
                  
  • start_times – time of day to run.
    • 
                  
  • run_calendar – which dates are valid.
    • 
                  
  • date_conditions: 1 – tells Autosys to obey the calendar rules.
    • 
                


                
Run on a specific date:

                

                  JIL – One‑time Date
                  insert_calendar: my_one_time_date

                  date: 07/15/2026


                  insert_job: my_job

                  job_type: c

                  command: /path/to/script.sh

                  machine: myserver

                  date_conditions: 1

                  run_calendar: my_one_time_date

                  start_times: "09:00"
                


                
Run on multiple specific dates:

                

                  JIL – Multiple Dates
                  insert_calendar: july_dates

                  date: 07/01/2026

                  date: 07/10/2026

                  date: 07/20/2026


                  insert_job: multi_date_job

                  job_type: c

                  command: /path/to/script.sh

                  machine: myserver

                  date_conditions: 1

                  run_calendar: july_dates

                  start_times: "08:00"
                


                
Run every weekday (Mon–Fri):

                

                  JIL – Weekdays
                  insert_job: weekday_job

                  job_type: c

                  command: /path/to/script.sh

                  machine: myserver

                  date_conditions: 1

                  run_calendar: workdays

                  start_times: "06:00"
                


                
🧠 Built‑in calendars like workdays and weekends are powerful shortcuts for common schedules.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🖱️
                  Web UI Path – Calendars & Schedule Tab
                

                WCC Calendar Manager
              

              

                
To create and attach calendars in WCC:

                
Create a specific‑dates calendar:

                
    
                  
  • WCC Home → Administration → Calendars.
    • 
                  
  • Click New Calendar.
    • 
                  
  • Select Specific Dates.
    • 
                  
  • Name: july_dates.
    • 
                  
  • Add dates: 07/15/2026, 07/20/2026, 07/30/2026.
    • 
                  
  • Save.
    • 
                


                
Attach calendar to a job:

                
    
                  
  • Edit the job → Schedule tab.
    • 
                  
  • Turn ON Date Conditions.
    • 
                  
  • Set Start Time (e.g., 09:00 AM).
    • 
                  
  • Select Run Calendar: july_dates.
    • 
                  
  • Save → Commit.
    • 
                


                
Exclude holidays:

                
    
                  
  • Create a holidays calendar with specific holiday dates.
    • 
                  
  • In the job’s Schedule tab, set Exclude Calendar to holidays.
    • 
                


                
🎯 “Run Calendar” defines when a job is allowed to run; “Exclude Calendar” defines when it must be skipped.

              

            

          


          

            

              

                

                  ⏱️
                  Advanced Intervals & Last‑Day Logic
                

                JIL Patterns
              

              

                
Run every 30 minutes between 08:00–17:00:

                

                  JIL – Every 30 Minutes
                  insert_job: half_hour_job

                  job_type: c

                  command: /path/to/script.sh

                  machine: myserver

                  date_conditions: 0

                  start_times: "08:00"

                  run_window: "08:00-17:00"

                  start_mins: 30
                


                
Run on the last day of the month:

                

                  JIL – Last Day
                  insert_calendar: last_day

                  monthdays: last


                  insert_job: month_end_job

                  job_type: c

                  command: /path/to/script.sh

                  machine: myserver

                  date_conditions: 1

                  run_calendar: last_day

                  start_times: "23:00"
                


                
📌 Use calendars for anything “date‑smart”: month‑end, quarter‑end, holidays, and special events.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🧮
                  Web UI – Calendar Types at a Glance
                

                Visual Builder
              

              

                
WCC supports several calendar types:

                
    
                  
  • Standard – basic recurring patterns.
    • 
                  
  • Specific Dates – exact dates like 07/15, 07/20.
    • 
                  
  • Monthly – 1st, 15th, last day, etc.
    • 
                  
  • Weekdays/Weekends – custom Mon–Sun selection.
    • 
                  
  • Holiday / Exclusion – used to skip specific dates.
    • 
                

                
🧩 Build calendars once and reuse them across hundreds of jobs for consistent scheduling rules.

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              

                📦
                Box Jobs & Workflow Design
              

              

                A box job is a container that groups related jobs and controls their schedule and lifecycle. It does not run commands itself; it orchestrates the jobs inside.
              

            

            

              🧱 Containers
              🔗 Dependencies
            

          


          

            

              

                

                  ⌨️
                  Command Path – Full Box JIL Workflow
                

                ETL Pipeline
              

              

                
Here is a classic ETL workflow using a box job and child jobs:

                

                  JIL – Box & Children
                  insert_job: DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX

                  job_type: b

                  box_success: 1

                  date_conditions: 1

                  run_calendar: workdays

                  start_times: "06:00"

                  description: "Daily processing workflow box"


                  insert_job: DUMMY_START

                  job_type: c

                  box_name: DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX

                  command: /bin/true

                  description: "Dummy job to start the workflow"


                  insert_job: EXTRACT_DATA

                  job_type: c

                  box_name: DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX

                  command: /opt/scripts/extract_data.sh

                  machine: server01

                  condition: s(DUMMY_START)

                  description: "Extract data from source system"


                  insert_job: TRANSFORM_DATA

                  job_type: c

                  box_name: DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX

                  command: /opt/scripts/transform_data.sh

                  machine: server01

                  condition: s(EXTRACT_DATA)

                  description: "Transform extracted data"


                  insert_job: LOAD_DATA

                  job_type: c

                  box_name: DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX

                  command: /opt/scripts/load_data.sh

                  machine: server02

                  condition: s(TRANSFORM_DATA)

                  description: "Load transformed data into target system"
                

                
📦 The box starts at 06:00 on workdays; the dummy fires instantly; then EXTRACT → TRANSFORM → LOAD in sequence.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🖱️
                  Web UI Path – Build the Same Box Visually
                

                WCC Flow
              

              

                
1. Create the Box:

                
    
                  
  • New → Job → Job Type: Box.
    • 
                  
  • General tab: Job Name DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX.
    • 
                  
  • Schedule tab: Date Conditions ON, Run Calendar workdays, Start Time 06:00.
    • 
                  
  • Save → Commit.
    • 
                


                
2. Create DUMMY_START:

                
    
                  
  • New → Job → Job Type: Command.
    • 
                  
  • General tab:
                    
      
                      
    • Job Name: DUMMY_START
      • 
                      
    • Box Name: DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX
      • 
                      
    • Command: /bin/true
      • 
                    
    
                  
    • 
                  
  • Save → Commit.
    • 
                


                
3. Create EXTRACT, TRANSFORM, LOAD:

                
    
                  
  • Each as a Command job with Box Name DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX.
    • 
                  
  • In the dependencies tab:
                    
      
                      
    • EXTRACT_DATA → condition: s(DUMMY_START).
      • 
                      
    • TRANSFORM_DATA → condition: s(EXTRACT_DATA).
      • 
                      
    • LOAD_DATA → condition: s(TRANSFORM_DATA).
      • 
                    
    
                  
    • 
                  
  • Save → Commit each job.
    • 
                


                
🕸️ Use the WCC job flow graph to show this chain to stakeholders — it clearly explains the ETL pipeline.

              

            

          


          

            

              

                

                  🧠
                  Box Behavior – Key Rules
                

                Concepts
              

              

                
Important box behavior rules:

                
    
                  
  • A box does not run commands; it only controls child jobs.
    • 
                  
  • A box has its own status, separate from its children.
    • 
                  
  • A box stays RUNNING until all child jobs complete.
    • 
                  
  • A box can be RUNNING while some jobs are ON_HOLD.
    • 
                  
  • A box can succeed even if some jobs never ran (conditions not met).
    • 
                

                
🧩 A box is a logical container, not a flowchart. Dependencies still live on each individual job.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🚨
                  Termination & Max Run Alarms
                

                Production Safety
              

              

                
Boxes and jobs can be configured to stop early if something goes wrong.

                
Max run alarm example:

                

                  JIL – max_run_alarm
                  insert_job: LONG_RUNNING_JOB

                  job_type: c

                  command: /opt/scripts/heavy_load.sh

                  machine: server01

                  date_conditions: 1

                  run_calendar: workdays

                  start_times: "01:00"

                  max_run_alarm: 60
                

                
This configuration raises an alarm if the job runs longer than 60 minutes.

                
🛡️ Max run alarms are essential in production to catch runaway jobs before they impact SLAs.

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              

                🔗
                REST API & CI/CD Automation
              

              

                The Autosys REST API exposes job definitions, events, status, and calendars over HTTP. It’s the foundation for CI/CD integration and automated job management.
              

            

            

              📡 AEWS /rest/v1
              🤖 CI/CD & DevOps
            

          


          

            

              

                

                  🌐
                  REST Basics – Endpoints & Auth
                

                Foundation
              

              

                
Typical base URL for Autosys Web Services (AEWS):

                

                  Base URL
                  https://<autosys-server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/
                

                
Authentication is usually Basic or token‑based:

                

                  Headers
                  Authorization: Basic QXV0b1N5czpQYXNzd29yZDEyMw==

                  Content-Type: application/json
                

                
🔐 In real environments, credentials are stored in secure vaults or CI/CD secrets, not hard‑coded.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🧱
                  Create / Update / Delete Jobs via REST
                

                Job Lifecycle
              

              

                
Create job (POST /jobs):

                

                  REST – Create Job
                  POST https://<server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/jobs


                  {

                    "jobName": "REST_EXTRACT_JOB",

                    "jobType": "CMD",

                    "command": "/opt/scripts/extract.sh",

                    "machine": "server01",

                    "owner": "autosys",

                    "dateConditions": true,

                    "startTimes": ["06:00"],

                    "runCalendar": "workdays",

                    "boxName": "DAILY_PROCESSING_BOX"

                  }
                


                
Update job (PUT /jobs/{jobName}):

                

                  REST – Update Job
                  PUT https://<server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/jobs/REST_EXTRACT_JOB


                  {

                    "command": "/opt/scripts/extract_v2.sh"

                  }
                


                
Delete job (DELETE /jobs/{jobName}):

                

                  REST – Delete Job
                  DELETE https://<server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/jobs/REST_EXTRACT_JOB
                

              

            

          


          

            

              

                

                  🚀
                  Start / Stop Jobs & Get Status
                

                Events & Status
              

              

                
Start job (FORCE_STARTJOB):

                

                  REST – Start Job
                  POST https://<server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/events


                  {

                    "eventType": "FORCE_STARTJOB",

                    "jobName": "REST_EXTRACT_JOB"

                  }
                


                
Kill job (KILLJOB):

                

                  REST – Kill Job
                  {

                    "eventType": "KILLJOB",

                    "jobName": "REST_EXTRACT_JOB"

                  }
                


                
Get job status:

                

                  REST – Job Status
                  GET https://<server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/jobs/REST_EXTRACT_JOB/status


                  {

                    "status": "SUCCESS",

                    "lastStart": "2026-06-20T06:00:00",

                    "lastEnd": "2026-06-20T06:02:14"

                  }
                

                
📊 Use these endpoints to feed dashboards and monitoring tools without scraping CLI output.

              

            


            

              

                

                  📆
                  Calendars via REST
                

                Date Intelligence
              

              

                
Create specific‑dates calendar:

                

                  REST – Create Calendar
                  POST https://<server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/calendars


                  {

                    "calendarName": "july_dates",

                    "type": "SPECIFIC",

                    "dates": ["2026-07-15", "2026-07-20", "2026-07-30"]

                  }
                


                
Attach to job:

                

                  REST – Attach Calendar
                  "runCalendar": "july_dates"
                


                
Query calendar:

                

                  REST – Get Calendar
                  GET https://<server>:9443/AEWS/rest/v1/calendars/july_dates
                


                
🧬 REST is ideal for CI/CD pipelines that create jobs and calendars automatically per environment.

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              

                🩺
                Statuses, sendevent, autorep & Real‑World Problems
              

              

                Knowing Autosys status codes and core commands is the key to diagnosing real production issues quickly and confidently.
              

            

            

              📊 SU • FA • RU • ST • OI • OH • QUE_WAIT
              🛠️ sendevent & autorep
            

          


          

            

              

                

                  📊
                  Job Status Codes & Core Commands
                

                Cheat Sheet
              

              

                
Key status codes:

                

                  Statuses
                  SU  → Success

                  FA  → Failure

                  RU  → Running

                  ST  → Starting

                  TE  → Terminated

                  OH  → On Hold

                  OI  → On Ice

                  INACTIVE → Not scheduled

                  QUE_WAIT → Waiting for resources

                  RESTART  → Restarting
                


                
Core control commands (sendevent):

                

                  sendevent
                  sendevent -E FORCE_STARTJOB -J jobname

                  sendevent -E KILLJOB -J jobname

                  sendevent -E JOB_ON_HOLD -J jobname

                  sendevent -E JOB_OFF_HOLD -J jobname

                  sendevent -E JOB_ON_ICE -J jobname

                  sendevent -E JOB_OFF_ICE -J jobname

                  sendevent -E CHANGE_STATUS -s SUCCESS -J jobname
                


                
Inspection commands (autorep & autoping):

                

                  autorep / autoping
                  autorep -J jobname -q   # Quick status

                  autorep -J jobname -r   # Run history

                  autorep -J jobname -d   # Full definition

                  autorep -J jobname -w   # Dependency tree


                  autoping -m machine     # Check agent connectivity
                

                
🧠 A solid troubleshooting pattern: start with autorep -J job -q and autoping -m machine to check status and connectivity.

              

            


            

              

                

                  🚑
                  11 Real‑World Problems & Fix Patterns
                

                Production Playbook
              

              

                
Common real‑world issues and their typical causes:

                
    
                  
  • Job stuck in STARTING – agent down, machine unreachable, script permissions wrong.
    • 
                  
  • Job stuck in QUE_WAIT – machine queue full, resource shortage, max concurrent jobs reached.
    • 
                  
  • Job didn’t run at scheduled time – wrong calendar, date_conditions=0, job ON_ICE, box not started.
    • 
                  
  • Job runs manually but not in Autosys – missing environment variables, PATH, or non‑interactive shell issues.
    • 
                  
  • Box stuck in RUNNING – child job ON_ICE, unmet conditions, QUE_WAIT, or inactive job.
    • 
                  
  • Job fails instantly – wrong script path, missing execute permission, wrong interpreter.
    • 
                  
  • Job succeeds too fast – empty script, wrong script referenced, permission issues.
    • 
                  
  • Job fails only in Autosys – environment variables missing, different user context.
    • 
                  
  • Calendar didn’t trigger – wrong type, not assigned, date_conditions=0, expired dates, timezone mismatch.
    • 
                  
  • Job stuck in TERMINATED – job killed, script hung, agent lost connection, machine rebooted.
    • 
                  
  • Dependencies not firing – wrong condition syntax, job name mismatch, parent ON_ICE, parent not SUCCESS, box not started.
    • 
                

                
🧪 Turn these into stories in interviews: “We had a job stuck in QUE_WAIT because the machine queue was full…”

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              

                🎯
                Autosys Mastery Quiz – 10 Questions
              

              

                Each question has four options. Click an answer to see if you’re thinking like a production Autosys engineer.
              

            

            

              🏆 Aim for 10/10
              🧠 Status • Calendars • Boxes • REST
            

          


          

            

              

                
                

                  

                    

                      1️⃣
                      What does date_conditions: 1 mean in a JIL job?
                    

                    Scheduling
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        It disables all calendars and runs only on start_times.
                      

                      

                        B
                        It tells Autosys to obey run_calendar and exclude_calendar rules.
                      

                      

                        C
                        It forces the job to run every minute.
                      

                      

                        D
                        It makes the job a file watcher.
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      2️⃣
                      Which statement about a BOX job is TRUE?
                    

                    Box Behavior
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        A box executes shell commands directly.
                      

                      

                        B
                        A box is a container that groups jobs and controls their schedule.
                      

                      

                        C
                        A box automatically runs all jobs in alphabetical order.
                      

                      

                        D
                        A box cannot have its own schedule.
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      3️⃣
                      What does status QUE_WAIT usually indicate?
                    

                    Status
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        The job is waiting for a file to arrive.
                      

                      

                        B
                        The job is waiting for machine resources or queue availability.
                      

                      

                        C
                        The job has completed successfully.
                      

                      

                        D
                        The job is on ice.
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      4️⃣
                      In WCC, where do you attach a calendar to a job?
                    

                    Web UI
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        In the General tab under “Owner”.
                      

                      

                        B
                        In the Schedule tab under “Run Calendar”.
                      

                      

                        C
                        In the Notifications tab.
                      

                      

                        D
                        In the Runtime tab under “Resources”.
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      5️⃣
                      What is the main difference between ON_HOLD and ON_ICE?
                    

                    Concept
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        ON_HOLD allows dependencies to propagate; ON_ICE does not.
                      

                      

                        B
                        ON_ICE allows dependencies to propagate; ON_HOLD does not.
                      

                      

                        C
                        ON_HOLD runs the job twice; ON_ICE runs it once.
                      

                      

                        D
                        There is no difference; they are synonyms.
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      6️⃣
                      Which REST endpoint starts a job (equivalent to FORCE_STARTJOB)?
                    

                    REST
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        POST /jobs/start
                      

                      

                        B
                        POST /AEWS/rest/v1/events with eventType = "FORCE_STARTJOB"
                      

                      

                        C
                        GET /jobs/{jobName}/run
                      

                      

                        D
                        PUT /jobs/{jobName}/status
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      7️⃣
                      Which JIL snippet correctly defines a file watcher job?
                    

                    Job Type
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        job_type: c; watch_file: /data/file.txt
                      

                      

                        B
                        job_type: f; watch_file: /data/file.txt; watch_interval: 60
                      

                      

                        C
                        job_type: b; watch_file: /data/file.txt
                      

                      

                        D
                        job_type: c; file_type: watcher
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      8️⃣
                      Which command shows a job’s dependency tree?
                    

                    CLI
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        autorep -J job -q
                      

                      

                        B
                        autorep -J job -w
                      

                      

                        C
                        sendevent -E JOB_ON_HOLD -J job
                      

                      

                        D
                        autoping -m machine
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      9️⃣
                      A job runs manually but fails in Autosys. What is the most likely cause?
                    

                    Troubleshooting
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        The script is too long.
                      

                      

                        B
                        Environment variables or PATH are missing in the Autosys execution context.
                      

                      

                        C
                        Autosys cannot run scripts at night.
                      

                      

                        D
                        The job type must be changed to BOX.
                      

                    

                  

                


                
                

                  

                    

                      🔟
                      Which calendar setup runs a job on the last day of every month?
                    

                    Calendars
                  

                  

                    

                      

                        A
                        run_calendar: workdays; start_times: "23:59"
                      

                      

                        B
                        insert_calendar: last_day; monthdays: last; run_calendar: last_day
                      

                      

                        C
                        date_conditions: 0; start_mins: 1440
                      

                      

                        D
                        job_type: f; watch_file: /tmp/lastday.flag
